author: han0110
content:
> I think for a while we could rely on something like  ethrex-<commit>/zisk-vX.X.X since requires no coordination and no other new standard that ELs and zkVMs must follow like in semver
Make sense

author: taulepton_
content: Ideally, I should be able to statically validate if a guest + zkvm combination is valid - I shouldn't have to wait for runtime errors / undefined behaviour, config validation should be able to evaluate this. Not a blocker but seems like low-hanging fruit that adds some nice properties. Does this add a lot of complexity on the versioning side of things?

author: jsign
content: I don't think we are arguing it is a bad idea nor that someone should wait to see runtime errors; but more like we might be a bit early now to do this. Plus there is no zkvm nor guest program today taht can meaningfully use semver. There is no zkevm nor el that fully complies to the standards today, so even if we try to do it now wouldn't be possible. I think this is a good idea but maybe now things aren't ready enough.

author: taulepton_
content: That sounds reasonable to me. If it's going to make dev/iteration awkward, let's defer. I will add an issue to the https://github.com/eth-act/execution-proofs-api repo to capture this. Sound reasonable?

author: jsign
content: yes please 🙏 . let's capture this idea somewhere because i think we will eventually do it, and we can direct people somewhere to get their feedback

author: jsign
content: <@402080271563751435> if you happen to write about this before wednesday lmk and i can mention it in the breakout call for people to take a look. if not, no rush and we can do it in the following call. (reg guest program versioning)

author: taulepton_
content: My plan was to just have an agent extract context from this convo and push it into an issue. I will do this today. Let me know if you had something else in mind or if you would like to take ownership of this effort (you have more context than I do).  Let me know what you think 🙂

author: jsign
content: feel free to delegate to agents 😆  i think is just writing a bit about the idea and should be fine. is mostly somthing that EL and zkVM teams must own reg being applied
